description: Bipartisan support for banning or forcing sale of TikTok grew from ~45% in early 2023 to 60%+ by early 2024, driven by national security concerns and Congressional hearings. Tests bipartisan consensus formation and generational opinion divide.

key_events.description: FBI Director Wray testifies TikTok poses national security threat. Bipartisan concern crystallizes around Chinese government data access.
TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew testifies before Congress (March 2023). Combative hearing boosts ban support across party lines.
Montana becomes first state to pass TikTok ban (later blocked by courts). Policy momentum builds.
House passes bipartisan RESTRICT Act forcing ByteDance divestiture. Senate consideration begins.
Biden signs TikTok divest-or-ban bill into law (April 2024). Supreme Court challenge anticipated. Support solidifies at ~60%.

calibration_notes: Tests bipartisan consensus formation on tech regulation. Key patterns: rare bipartisan agreement (both parties >55% support), stark generational divide (18-29 only ~35% support vs 65+ at ~80%), national security framing overcoming free speech objections, steady monotonic growth rather than shock-driven shift, opposition remained stable (~28%) while undecideds converted to support. Pew Research, Reuters/Ipsos polling data used.
